2. How many 2/3 cup servings can you get out of 5 cups of ice cream?

problems.label: 3a
problems.group: 3. Solve these problems by reasoning logically. Write a division sentence for each problem.
problems.display: There is 1 4/6 of a pizza left over and two people share it equally. How much does each one get?

problems.label: 3b
problems.group: 3. Solve these problems by reasoning logically. Write a division sentence for each problem.
problems.display: There is 9/10 of a cake left over and three people share it equally. How much does each one get?

problems.label: 4a
problems.group: 4. The picture shows how much pie is left. That amount is divided among a certain number of people. How much does each person get? See workbook.

5. Three people share a 1/4-kg chocolate bar equally. How many kilograms will each of them get?
6. Five siblings inherited a plot of land that measures 2 4/10 hectares. If they divide the plot equally, what portion of a hectare will each one get?

problems.label: 7a
problems.group: 7. A recipe calls for 2/3 cup of wheat flour for each full recipe. How many batches of the recipe can she make if she has...

problems.label: 7b
problems.group: 7. A recipe calls for 2/3 cup of wheat flour for each full recipe. How many batches of the recipe can she make if she has...
8. An airport takes up a rectangular area that is 3 2/5 kilometres long and 4/5 kilometre wide. What is its area? Use fractions.

problems.label: 9a
problems.group: 9. Jeannie likes to eat 2/5 of a package of crackers in one sitting. How many times can she do that, if she has three packages?
problems.display: Which calculation can be used to solve the problem?

problems.label: 9b
problems.group: 9. Jeannie likes to eat 2/5 of a package of crackers in one sitting. How many times can she do that, if she has three packages?
problems.display: Do the calculation. What does a fractional result mean in this context?

problems.label: 10a
problems.group: 10. A gym class takes 3/4 of an hour.
problems.display: How many such classes could you fit, end-to-end, in five hours?

problems.label: 10b
problems.group: 10. A gym class takes 3/4 of an hour.
problems.display: If those classes start at 9 AM, when will the last class end?

problems.label: 11a
problems.group: 11. Mary's vegetable garden is 2 1/2 metres by 2 1/2 metres. She divided her garden into quarters. See workbook.
problems.display: Which calculation is correct (or are several)?

problems.label: 11b
problems.group: 11. Mary's vegetable garden is 2 1/2 metres by 2 1/2 metres. She divided her garden into quarters. See workbook.
problems.display: Which one is the most useful or natural? Why?

problems.label: 12a
problems.group: 12. An airport runway is 3 1/5 kilometres long, and takes up 4/25 square kilometre in area. How wide is it?

problems.label: 12b
problems.group: 12. An airport runway is 3 1/5 kilometres long, and takes up 4/25 square kilometre in area. How wide is it?
13. The sides of a rectangle are in a ratio of 2:3, and its perimeter is 1 1/4 metres. What are the lengths of its sides? Hint: draw a sketch.

problems.label: Puzzle a
problems.group: Puzzle Corner. How many 3/8-metre long pieces can you cut out of 11 metres of ribbon?

problems.display: How long is the piece that is left over?
